response: Based on the context provided, Tymnet was an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, California, that utilized virtual call packet switched technology. It used X.25, SNA/SDLC, BSC, and ASCII interfaces to connect host computers (servers) at thousands of large companies, educational institutions, and government agencies. Users typically connected via dial-up or dedicated async connections. The business consisted of a large public network supporting dial-up users and a private network business that allowed government agencies and large companies (primarily banks and airlines) to build their own dedicated networks, which were often connected via gateways to the public network. Tymnet was also connected to dozens of other public networks in the U.S. and internationally via X.25/X.75 gateways.
expected: an international data communications network headquartered in San Jose, CA
